epics.title: Epic 1: Shadow Setup
epics.theme: Download Your Shadow - Setup wizard with mandatory local AI model download gatekeeper. User completes onboarding flow with WiFi detection, progress tracking, and model verification before accessing the app.
acceptance_criteria: WHO can ACTION CONTEXT
User can view welcome screen with 'Download Your Shadow' branding on app first launch
System validates WiFi connection and blocks cellular downloads before model download
User can view progress bar updating every 5% during model download
User can view estimated time remaining during download
System resumes download after app restart or interruption
System validates model checksum after download completion
System prevents app usage until model download completes successfully
User can view 'Your Shadow is Ready' confirmation screen after successful download
System handles download failures with retry UX and exponential backoff
epics.human_review_deliverable: User completes onboarding and downloads local AI model before accessing app. App blocks all features until model download succeeds on WiFi.

tasks.title: CLR-001: Setup Wizard Flow Implementation

must: Translate 4 HTML mockups to React Native (welcome, wifi, download, complete)
Implement WiFi detection and block cellular downloads
Implement progress tracking with 5% updates
Handle app state changes (background/foreground)
Use BottomSheetInput for all text inputs per CLAUDE.md guidelines
never: Allow model download on cellular connection
Let user bypass setup wizard without completing download
strictly: Download MUST only work on WiFi
App MUST block usage until model verified

specification.objective: Build setup wizard onboarding flow with WiFi detection, progress tracking, and download state persistence
specification.success_state: User completes 4-step setup wizard (welcome → wifi check → download → complete) with model downloaded and verified, app unlocked for use

acceptance_criteria.given: User launches app for first time
acceptance_criteria.then: Welcome screen displays with 'Download Your Shadow' branding
System validates WiFi connection and blocks cellular downloads
acceptance_criteria.verify: await expect(page.getByText('WiFi required. Cellular downloads disabled.')).toBeVisible()

acceptance_criteria.then: Progress bar updates every 5% with estimated time remaining

acceptance_criteria.when: App goes to background or restarts
acceptance_criteria.then: Download resumes automatically from last progress point
Checksum validation passes and 'Your Shadow is Ready' screen displays

write_prohibited: app.json - WiFi detection uses React Native NetInfo API

interaction_notes: Welcome → WiFi check → Download → Complete (linear flow)
App state changes trigger resume logic
design.anti_pattern: Do NOT allow cellular downloads or bypass setup wizard

test_criteria.statement: Welcome screen displays with Download Your Shadow branding when app launches for first time
WiFi detection blocks cellular downloads when user attempts download on cellular connection
Progress bar updates every 5% with estimated time remaining during model download
Download resumes from last progress point when app restarts or returns from background
Model checksum validation passes and Your Shadow is Ready screen displays when download completes

tasks.assignment_rationale: React Native UI implementation with 4 screens, WiFi detection, progress tracking, and state persistence

tasks.title: CLR-002: Local Model Integration

must: Install MLX framework for Apple Silicon
Create native module bridge for React Native
Implement model download manager with retry logic
Implement in-memory model caching (singleton pattern)
Implement checksum validation
never: Load model multiple times in memory
strictly: Model MUST download to native file system
Model MUST cache as singleton in memory

specification.objective: Integrate Qwen3.5 0.8B local model with MLX runtime, native module bridge, download manager, and in-memory caching
specification.success_state: Model downloads from CDN to native file system, validates with checksum, loads as singleton in memory, and generates leg labels in <0.5s

acceptance_criteria.then: Model file saves to native file system with progress tracking
SHA256 checksum matches expected value
Model loads as singleton (only one instance in memory)
MLX inference runs in Swift/Kotlin and returns labels

write_prohibited: AsyncStorage - model too large for async storage

interaction_notes: Native module bridge required (MLX not JS-compatible)
Singleton pattern prevents duplicate model loading
design.anti_pattern: Do NOT try to run MLX in JavaScript - requires native bridge

test_criteria.statement: Model file saves to native file system when download manager fetches from CDN
SHA256 checksum matches expected value when model download completes
Model loads as singleton with only one instance in memory when model loader checks cache
MLX inference returns leg labels when native module bridge invoked
test_criteria.verify: curl -X POST http://localhost:8081/generateLegLabels -d '{"routes":[...]}' | jq '.labels | length' | grep -q '2'

tasks.assignment_rationale: Native module development, MLX framework integration, model download manager, and file system operations

tasks.title: CLR-003: Gatekeeper Implementation

must: Block all app features until model download completes
Show 'Setup Required' screen if model missing
Persist download state across app restarts
Handle download failures with retry UX
never: Show main app without model verification
strictly: App MUST block usage until model verified
Download state MUST persist across restarts

specification.objective: Implement gatekeeper logic that blocks app usage until model download completes, with persistent state and retry UX
specification.success_state: App cannot be used without verified model, download state persists across restarts, failures show retry UX

acceptance_criteria.then: Setup Required screen displays blocking main app
Download state persists and resume option available
Retry button displays with error message

acceptance_criteria.when: Gatekeeper check runs on app launch
acceptance_criteria.then: Main app accessible without setup screen

interaction_notes: Gatekeeper check runs on app launch
Main app blocked until verification passes
design.anti_pattern: Do NOT allow any app usage without model verification

test_criteria.statement: Setup Required screen displays blocking main app when app launches without verified model
Download state persists and resume option available when app restarts during model download
Retry button displays with error message when model download fails
Main app accessible without setup screen when model verified and ready

tasks.assignment_rationale: Gatekeeper logic, React Native UI implementation, state persistence, and error handling

tasks.title: CLR-004: Model Download Persistence

must: Persist download state to AsyncStorage
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff
Handle corrupted model cache
Implement resume after interruption
never: Store download state in volatile memory
strictly: Download state MUST persist to AsyncStorage

specification.objective: Implement persistent download state with retry logic, exponential backoff, and corruption recovery
specification.success_state: Download state persists across app restarts, retries with exponential backoff, corrupted cache triggers re-download

acceptance_criteria.then: Progress percentage saved with timestamp
Retry attempts with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s)
Corrupted cache deleted and re-download triggered
Download resumes from last progress point

interaction_notes: Resume after interruption uses saved progress
design.anti_pattern: Do NOT retry without exponential backoff or store state in memory

test_criteria.statement: Progress percentage saved with timestamp when download state persists to AsyncStorage
Retry attempts with exponential backoff when download fails and retry logic triggers
Corrupted cache deleted and re-download triggered when checksum validation fails
Download resumes from last progress point when app restarts and state loaded from AsyncStorage

tasks.assignment_rationale: AsyncStorage persistence, retry logic, exponential backoff implementation, and corruption recovery

epics.title: Epic 2: Map Foundation
epics.theme: See the World - Basic Mapbox rendering with theme-aware styling, camera controls, markers, and polylines. User sees Mapbox map in dark/light themes with pan/zoom capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: WHO can ACTION CONTEXT
User can view Mapbox map on route planning screen
System displays dark style when system theme is dark
System displays light style when system theme is light
System updates map style immediately when system theme changes
User can pan map by dragging with touch input
User can zoom map by pinching with touch input
System can center map on specific coordinates programmatically
System can fit camera to route bounds automatically
Camera movements are smooth and animated
Camera respects minimum and maximum zoom levels
User can view route polylines rendered on map
User can view start/end location markers on map
User can view waypoint markers on map
Polylines display correct colors for weather conditions
Polylines display appropriate stroke widths
Markers display at correct coordinates
Shapes update when route data changes
System can convert latitude/longitude objects to Mapbox array format
System can convert Mapbox array format to latitude/longitude objects
Route polylines render at correct geographic locations
Markers display at correct geographic locations
No coordinate drift or offset errors in rendering
Conversion utilities have unit tests with edge cases
human_test_steps: Open app and see map render
View route polyline with correct colors
epics.human_review_deliverable: User sees themed Mapbox map with camera controls, markers, and polylines. Map renders in dark/light themes, camera controls work (zoom/pan/fit), markers and polylines display correctly.

must: Install @rnmapbox/maps v10.1.0 via npm
Read existing package.json to avoid conflicts

specification.objective: Install @rnmapbox/maps package and verify compatibility with existing dependencies
specification.success_state: @rnmapbox/maps v10.1.0 installed successfully, npm install completes without conflicts

acceptance_criteria.then: @rnmapbox/maps v10.1.0 installed without errors
No conflicts with existing packages
acceptance_criteria.verify: npm ls --depth=0 | grep -q 'UNMET DEPENDENCY' && exit 1 || exit 0

write_prohibited: components/** - no code changes in INFRA task

design.anti_pattern: Do NOT install beta or unstable versions

test_criteria.statement: @rnmapbox/maps v10.1.0 installed without errors when npm install runs
No conflicts with existing packages when dependency tree checked

must: Add Mapbox plugin to app.json or app.config.ts
Configure RNMapboxMapsImpl and download token
Read existing Expo config before modifying
never: Hardcode Mapbox access tokens in config files (use environment variables)
strictly: Plugin MUST be added to Expo config array

specification.objective: Configure @rnmapbox/maps Expo plugin with Mapbox tokens and test build on iOS and Android
specification.success_state: Expo plugin configured, app builds successfully on iOS simulator and Android emulator

acceptance_criteria.then: Plugin array includes @rnmapbox/maps configuration
Build completes successfully for iOS simulator
acceptance_criteria.verify: xcodebuild -scheme LaneShadow -sdk iphonesimulator -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15' build 2>&1 | grep -q 'BUILD SUCCEEDED'

acceptance_criteria.then: Build completes successfully for Android emulator

write_prohibited: components/** - no code changes in INFRA task

design.anti_pattern: Do NOT hardcode tokens in config files

test_criteria.statement: Plugin array includes @rnmapbox/maps configuration when Expo config updated
Build completes successfully for iOS simulator when npx expo run:ios executes
Build completes successfully for Android emulator when npx expo run:android executes

tasks.title: CLR-007: Create MapboxMapView Wrapper

must: Read existing components/map/map-view.tsx to understand current MapView wrapper
Use semantic theme for style URL mapping
Support both dark and light map styles
Implement coordinate conversion utilities ([lat, lng] → [lng, lat])
Keep component under 300 lines
never: Hardcode Mapbox access tokens in component files
Use Google Maps coordinate format [lat, lng] without conversion
strictly: Coordinates MUST flip to [lng, lat] for Mapbox

specification.objective: Build MapboxMapView wrapper component with camera controls, theme support, and coordinate conversion
specification.success_state: MapboxMapView renders map with camera positioning, marker support, polyline rendering, and theme-aware style URLs

acceptance_criteria.then: Map renders with camera at initial position
Map uses dark-v11 style URL
All markers display at correct coordinates
Error boundary catches and displays fallback

write_prohibited: app.json - token config requires separate task

interaction_notes: Camera responds to programmatic position changes
Theme switches trigger style URL updates
design.anti_pattern: Do NOT use Google Maps [lat, lng] format - MUST flip to [lng, lat]

test_criteria.statement: MapboxMapView component renders when mounted with valid props
Dark theme style URL is applied when semantic theme is dark
All markers render at flipped coordinates when markers array provided
Error boundary displays fallback UI when invalid coordinates provided

tasks.assignment_rationale: React Native component with camera controls, theme support, and coordinate conversion

epics.title: Epic 3: Offline Regions
epics.theme: Prepare for Adventure - Offline region download and management. User downloads, manages, and deletes offline map regions with progress tracking and storage warnings.
acceptance_criteria: WHO can ACTION CONTEXT
User can access offline region download screen from settings
User can define region bounds using map interface or coordinate input
User can name the downloaded region
User can initiate download with confirmation dialog
System downloads map tiles for specified region
System stores region metadata in Convex
Download completes successfully with valid region data
User can cancel download before completion
User can view progress bar during download
Progress bar updates smoothly as download progresses
System displays percentage complete (0-100%)
System displays estimated time remaining
System displays downloaded file size
Progress indicator dismisses when download completes
User can background download and return to see progress
User can access offline regions list from settings
System displays all downloaded regions in list format
Each region shows name, geographic bounds, and size
Each region shows download date
List is sorted by most recently used
List updates when regions are added or removed
Empty state displays helpful message when no regions downloaded
User can select region for deletion from list
System displays confirmation dialog before deletion
Confirmation dialog shows region size to inform decision
System removes region data from device storage
System removes region metadata from Convex
List updates to remove deleted region
System updates available storage space
User can cancel deletion without losing data
System checks available storage before download
System warns user if insufficient space for requested region
Warning message shows required space vs available space
System prevents download when space insufficient
System suggests deleting old regions to free space
System handles storage errors during download gracefully
System cleans up partial downloads on failure
User can access offline help from settings or download screen
Help content explains what offline routing is
Help content explains how to download regions
Help content explains when offline routing is active
Help content includes FAQ for common questions
Help content is accessible and easy to understand
User can dismiss help content and return to previous screen
human_test_steps: See list of downloaded regions (empty initially)
See region in list with size and date
See storage warning when insufficient space
epics.human_review_deliverable: User downloads, manages, and deletes offline map regions. Region download screen accessible from settings, bounding box selection works, download progress tracks smoothly, regions list shows metadata, delete confirmation works, storage warnings display correctly.

tasks.title: CLR-008: Offline Region Download Manager

must: Use Mapbox OfflineManager API for pack downloads
Implement progress tracking with percentage and ETA
Handle download cancellation and resume
Store region metadata in Convex
Check available storage before download
never: Store region data in AsyncStorage (too large)
strictly: Storage check MUST run before download

specification.objective: Implement offline region download manager using Mapbox OfflineManager API with progress tracking, storage checks, and Convex metadata storage
specification.success_state: User can download map regions with progress tracking, regions persist across app restarts, storage warnings display correctly

acceptance_criteria.then: Mapbox OfflineManager starts pack download
Percentage and ETA display every 5%
Warning shows required vs available space
Region stored in Convex with name, bounds, size, date

design.anti_pattern: Do NOT download without storage check or store pack data in Convex

test_criteria.statement: Mapbox OfflineManager starts pack download when user selects region bounds and download initiated
Percentage and ETA display every 5% when download in progress and progress updates
Warning shows required vs available space when storage insufficient and download attempted
Region stored in Convex with name, bounds, size, date when download completes and metadata saved

tasks.assignment_rationale: Mapbox OfflineManager API integration, progress tracking, and Convex metadata storage

tasks.title: CLR-009: Region Selection UI

must: Translate 4 HTML mockups to React Native (region download, progress, list, delete)
Implement bounding box selection on map
Implement regions list with swipe-to-delete
Use BottomSheetInput for all text inputs per CLAUDE.md

specification.objective: Build offline region management UI screens for region selection, download progress, regions list, and delete confirmation
specification.success_state: User can select region bounds on map, name region, download with progress, view list, and delete with confirmation

acceptance_criteria.then: Map shows with bounding box selection controls

acceptance_criteria.given: User defines bounds and enters name
acceptance_criteria.then: Download progress screen shows with percentage
Each region shows name, bounds, size, date
Dialog displays region size and confirm/cancel buttons

interaction_notes: Bounding box selection with drag corners
design.anti_pattern: Do NOT allow download without name or delete without confirmation

test_criteria.statement: Map shows with bounding box selection controls when user opens offline maps settings and region selection screen displays
Download progress screen shows with percentage when user defines bounds, enters name, and confirms download
Each region shows name, bounds, size, date when user views regions list and list renders
Dialog displays region size and confirm/cancel buttons when user swipes region to delete and confirmation dialog shows

tasks.assignment_rationale: React Native UI implementation with 4 screens, map interaction, and list management
